body.value: 人口 六萬六千四百六十三人
人口 (じんこう) は六万六千四百六十三人

Total population (jinkō) = 66,463 persons. Combined with the 39% samurai-class share (士族+卒族 = 26,211 / 66,463), Chōshū is unusually samurai-dense — consistent with its post-1860s mobilization for the Restoration.
66,463 persons
人口
戸數 一萬七千七百十四
戸数 (こすう) は一万七千七百十四戸

Total households = 17,714. Cross-check: 66,463 / 17,714 = 3.75 persons per household — a low ratio characteristic of samurai-heavy registries where individual stipend-receiving households are counted separately even if part of a single residence.
17,714 households
戸數
内 士族 人口 一萬五千八百四十九人
うち士族 (しぞく) の人口は一万五千八百四十九人

Of which: shizoku (samurai-class) population = 15,849 persons. The 内 prefix marks this as a SUB-COUNT of the total. This labeling pattern (内 X 人口) was the gap that caused Chōshū's HI shizoku data to be missing from the analytic dataset until the 2026-05-27 build-script fix added 内 X 人口 to the whitelist.
15,849 persons
内 士族 人口
内 卒族 人口 一萬三百六十二人
うち卒族 (そつぞく) の人口は一万三百六十二人

Of which: sotsu-class (lower samurai / ashigaru) population = 10,362 persons. Same 内 prefix convention as the shizoku row. This figure cross-validates exactly with the primary-source Meiji Shiyō entry (10,362).
10,362 persons
内 卒族 人口
